description: Site section banner region containing the section title bar and hierarchical desktop section navigation for the current section of the university website.
selectorsCovered: .region-banner .paragraph--type--para-section-nav > nav > ul > li > ul > li > ul
.region-banner .paragraph--type--para-section-nav > nav > ul > li > ul > li > ul > li

props.name: items
props.type: Array<{ label: string; href?: string; isUosLink?: boolean; children?: Array<{ label: string; href?: string; isUosLink?: boolean; children?: Array<{ label: string; href?: string; isUosLink?: boolean }> }> }>

stories.name: Default
stories.description: Management School section navigation with multiple top-level categories

notes: The root div carries both 'region' and 'region-banner' classes. The internal Drupal structure wraps the section-navigation view which contains: (1) a views-field-label slot rendered as a SectionTitle molecule (div.section-title-wrapper inside div.section-title.row), and (2) a views-field-field-section-navigation slot rendered as a paragraph block containing nav.desktop-section-navigation. Top-level li items may be text-only labels (no href) acting as category groupings. Some links carry class 'uoslink' as an additional modifier. The js-view-dom-id-* class on div.section-navigation is dynamic and must not be hardcoded. The block-views wrapper has id='block-views-block-section-navigation-block-1' which should be hardcoded as it is consistent across pages.
